behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure is most likely at the module handoff boundary (history selection -> LLM request builder), not just inside a pure list-slicing helper. An integration test wiring conversation-history logic with prompt/request construction can catch wrong ordering, off-by-one trimming, or missing trim before send. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only requires checking what is passed to the LLM, not real network/subprocess (disqualifies System), not model-output quality (disqualifies Agentic), and no required multi-agentic chain (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire the history manager and LLM request-construction modules in-process. Feed a history longer than 10 messages with identifiable sequence IDs, execute the send path, and assert the outbound LLM payload contains exactly the most recent 10 in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment/generation quality; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide relevance failures. This is a single model-quality check, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one generated response quality outcome, not a required multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompts against the production model interface with a labeled set of customer questions and relevance rubrics. Score whether responses directly answer each question (not generic/off-topic), and fail when relevance drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires multiple agentic steps in sequence: initial generation, tone judgment, then regeneration trigger and second generation. A single-step Agentic test is insufficient to catch chain-level failures (e.g., correct detection but no re-prompt, or wrong branch wiring). Rejected lower levels: deterministic mocks can validate routing but cannot validate real tone-judgment correctness (disqualifies Integration/System as minimum).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real model calls: provide casual-tier inputs likely to elicit formal first drafts, verify tone-check flags formality, verify regeneration is invoked, and verify regenerated output is produced through the regeneration branch.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module persistence correctness (chat flow + repository/DB layer + timestamp mapping). Integration with a real local DB catches schema/transaction/serialization issues. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real external network/service behavior beyond local DB interaction. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness target is persistence integrity, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chat service with a test/local DB, exercise a case with regeneration, then query persisted rows/events and assert all artifacts are recorded (user message, first draft, regenerated draft/final) with expected timestamp fields and ordering.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is orchestration between tone-check result handling, response state replacement, and delivery layer. This can be caught by wiring modules with controlled deterministic doubles for model outputs and asserting only final response is exposed. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require evaluating model quality itself (disqualifies Agentic as minimum) and does not require validating full multi-step model quality chain, only replacement semantics after trigger (disqualifies Workflow as minimum).
behaviors.test_description: Integrate response orchestrator, tone-check branch handling, and outbound delivery/API layer. Force a regeneration-trigger path with controlled first/second drafts, then assert customer-facing payload/stream includes only the regenerated response and excludes the rejected draft.
